description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: init and test, using the default Docker image. In the init stage add three jobs: ssh_init runs the inline function ssh_init "$TEST_PRIVATE_KEY" "$TEST_HOST_NAME" then executes ssh $TEST_USER_NAME@$TEST_HOST_NAME "echo logged-in !"; ssh_init_sh runs curl -sL https://gitlab.com/gitlab-cd/ssh-template/raw/master/ssh-init.sh | bash and then calls ssh_run "$TEST_USER_NAME" "$TEST_HOST_NAME" "$TEST_PRIVATE_KEY" "echo logged-in !" with variables SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS set to $TEST_HOST_NAME and SSH_PRIVATE_KEY set to $TEST_PRIVATE_KEY; ssh_init_sh_no_host also curls the script and runs ssh_run with the same arguments but only defines SSH_PRIVATE_KEY. In the test stage add a ssh_run job that directly runs ssh_run "$TEST_USER_NAME" "$TEST_HOST_NAME" "$TEST_PRIVATE_KEY" "echo logged-in !". Include a before_script that defines the ssh_init and ssh_run functions used by all jobs, and reference the variables TEST_PRIVATE_KEY, TEST_HOST_NAME, TEST_USER_NAME, SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS and SSH_PRIVATE_KEY. No caches, artifacts, or special rules are configured.
